No directly relevant papers. I'll proceed from the well-established Knowles/Cohen principle: lag time scales as a power of monomer concentration (t_lag ∝ m0^(-γ)), so nucleation flux must be a strong increasing function of m0. The breakthrough idea encodes this via a supersaturation ratio. The physical principle is sound — I'll implement it.

The key insight: a state-c-only tanh gate fires at the same `c` for every curve, so it cannot produce different lag *durations*. Replacing it with an m0-dependent supersaturation nucleation flux lets the source rate (and hence integrated lag duration) vary strongly with m0, which is exactly what controls held-out timing shape loss.

Let me implement the breakthrough idea, removing tanh entirely.
